functions: Tumor suppressor
Transcription factor regulating apoptosis and cell cycle
Mediator of p53 pathway activation downstream of MDM2 inhibition
Regulator of cellular response to oxidative stress and DNA damage
pubmed.druggability_notes: TP53 is targeted indirectly via MDM2-p53 antagonists. Brigimadlin (Brightline-1 trial) and the related agent class activate the TP53 pathway in MDM2-amplified dedifferentiated liposarcoma. MDM2 amplification serves as a selection biomarker for these antagonists, though no predictive efficacy biomarkers were identified. Network pharmacology/molecular docking studies (quercetin, luteolin) report moderate predicted binding affinities to p53-related targets in a heart failure context.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that induces cell cycle arrest, DNA repair, or apoptosis upon binding target DNA
Tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest or apoptosis depending on cell type and physiological context
Negatively regulates cell division by controlling expression of genes including cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitors
Induces apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression or repression of Bcl-2
Cooperates with mitochondrial PPIF to activate oxidative stress-induced necrosis, largely independent of transcription
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity when associated with the CAK complex in response to DNA damage, halting cell cycle progression
Induces transcription of long intergenic non-coding RNAs (lincRNA-p21, lincRNA-Mkln1) involved in apoptosis and cell-cycle regulation
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transcriptional activation of PER2
pathways: DNA damage response, signal transduction by p53 class mediator
Intrinsic apoptotic signaling pathway by p53 class mediator
Intrinsic apoptotic signaling pathway in response to DNA damage by p53 class mediator
Intrinsic apoptotic signaling pathway in response to endoplasmic reticulum stress
Mitotic G1 DNA damage checkpoint signaling
cellular_states: Cells undergoing cellular and replicative senescence
Cells responding to hypoxia, gamma/ionizing radiation, and UV
uniprot.druggability_notes: Interacts with MDM2/MDM4 family proteins (ubiquitin ligase binding) which regulate p53 stability — a well-established therapeutic axis. 3D-structure available. Zinc/metal-binding (copper ion binding) and DNA-binding domains noted. Pro-apoptotic activity modulated by ASPP family interactions (PPP1R13B/ASPP1, TP53BP2/ASPP2 activating; PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibitory), representing potential modulation points.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that induces cell cycle arrest, DNA repair or apoptosis upon target DNA binding
Tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest or apoptosis depending on physiological context and cell type
Negatively regulates cell division by controlling expression of cell-cycle genes including a cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor (p21)
Induces apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression or repression of Bcl-2; pro-apoptotic activity activated by ASPP1/ASPP2 and inhibited by iASPP
Activates oxidative stress-induced necrosis in cooperation with mitochondrial PPIF, largely independent of transcription
Induces transcription of lincRNA-p21 mediating TP53-dependent transcriptional repression and apoptosis
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity in CAK complex in response to DNA damage, halting cell cycle progression
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transcriptional activation of PER2

disease_associations.disease: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

opentargets.druggability_notes: Loss of function of TP53 in cancer due to loss of tetramerization ability (R-HSA-9723905) is noted; the tetramerization domain represents a structural vulnerability. No explicit small-molecule binding pocket or drug class is described in the provided text.
